dependencies: Purchases from thousands of suppliers, both domestic and international
No single supplier accounts for more than 10% of purchases
Consolidated product procurement program for branded merchandise and private label products
International procurement through global procurement centers
Supply chain vulnerable to conditions beyond control including supplier labor shortages, weather, climate change, transportation interruptions, product recalls, geopolitical events
risks: Supplier inability to provide products due to labor shortages, work stoppages, strikes
Weather conditions and climate change impacts on supply
Transportation interruptions including ocean cargo container shortages
Geopolitical events such as invasion of Ukraine by Russia and Israel-Hamas War
Increased product costs from supplier cost inflation
Supply chain disruptions from international political/financial instability, military conflicts, trade restrictions, tariffs
Dependence on third-party suppliers for product availability

regulatory_risks: Federal Food, Drug and Cosmetic Act and FDA regulations on food safety and quality
Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) requirements for prevention-based controls
Federal Meat Inspection Act and Poultry Products Inspection Act (USDA regulations)
Perishable Agricultural Commodities Act and Packers and Stockyard Act
Public Health Security and Bioterrorism Preparedness and Response Act of 2002
FDA registration and inspection requirements for facilities
Federal Trade Commission standards for advertising and marketing claims
Department of Labor employment practice standards and regulations
Department of Transportation trucking operations regulations (Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration, Federal Highway Administration)
Federal Aviation Administration regulations for air transport
False Claims Act and similar state statutes for government contracts
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) anti-bribery and accounting requirements
Sherman Antitrust Act and related antitrust laws
European Union regulations on competition, product composition, packaging, labeling, advertisement, food safety
General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) for data privacy in EU
U.K. Modern Slavery Act 2015 supply chain reporting requirements
U.K. Bribery Act 2010 anti-corruption requirements
Environmental protection statutes for water discharge, air emissions, waste disposal, refrigeration systems, fuel storage tanks
Occupational Safety and Health Act (OSHA) manufacturing and safety standards
National Labor Relations Act governing collective bargaining
Fair Labor Standards Act for minimum wages and overtime
Title VII of the Civil Rights Act and Americans with Disabilities Act for employment discrimination
Workers' compensation program regulations
State and local health agency regulations and licensing requirements
Compliance requirements for government contractor relationships (Department of Defense, Department of Veterans Affairs)
Potential changes in labor legislation affecting unionization and collective bargaining
Tax law changes and regulations in multiple jurisdictions
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax rules (15% minimum rate effective fiscal 2025)
Climate change and greenhouse gas emissions regulations

sanction_risks: Export controls and financial/economic sanctions imposed by governments in response to military conflicts such as invasion of Ukraine by Russia
Sanctions on certain industry sectors and parties impacting operations
Trade barriers or restrictions on global trade from geopolitical tensions
